title: Vim Guide for Lazy Devs
description: A zero-fluff, pure command and shortcut reference guide for developers on Linux. This book bypasses long explanations, historical context, and theoretical concepts to deliver instant keyboard muscle memory for maximum text-editing speed.

chapters.title: Hard Survival & Leaving Vim
chapters.description: The absolute bare minimum commands required to handle files without breaking things. Covers opening files, safe saving, forcing a quick exit, and recovering from unexpected terminal crashes by handling leftover .swp files.

chapters.title: High-Speed Navigation & Jump Lists
chapters.description: Moving through a codebase without touching the arrow keys. Covers basic HJKL movements, word and token leaps (w, b, e, W, B, E), line anchors (0, ^, $, g_), screen scrolling (Ctrl+d/u, zt, zz, zb), matching bracket jumps (%), single-line character searches (f, F, t, T), and traversing the jump history list (Ctrl+o, Ctrl+i).

chapters.title: Precision Editing & Text Operations
chapters.description: Efficient text modification shortcuts. Covers instant insert mode variants (i, I, a, A, o, O), character replacements (x, r, R, s, ~), delete and change operators paired with motion (dw, d$, dd, cw, ce, C), yank/paste registers, and using the undo/redo/repeat mechanics (u, Ctrl+r, .).

chapters.title: System Clipboard Interactivity
chapters.description: Bridging the gap between Vim and the Linux desktop. Focuses explicitly on targeting the system clipboard registers to seamlessly copy and paste code blocks (+y, +p) directly between Vim and external browser tabs, StackOverflow snippets, or communication tools.

chapters.title: Power Tactics: Text Objects & Macros
chapters.description: Advanced modifications utilizing code structure. Covers rapid changes inside or around structural delimiters like parentheses, brackets, and quotes (ciw, di(, di", ya{), alongside recording, executing, and repeating automated macros (q, @, @@) for repetitive syntax modifications.

chapters.title: Search, Destroy, & Global Replace
chapters.description: Locating and modifying code patterns instantly. Covers targeted phrase searching (/, ?, n, N), instant matching of the word under the cursor (*, #), global search-and-replace strings across entire files (:s, :%s, /g, /gc), and global case-insensitivity overrides (:set ic, \c).

chapters.title: Advanced Visual Mode & Column Editing
chapters.description: Manipulating large sections of text at once. Covers visual character and line highlights (v, V, gv), alongside visual block mode (Ctrl+v) for vertical multi-line column insertions, bullet alignments, and structural indentation shifts.

chapters.title: Windows, Tabs, & Buffers
chapters.description: Managing a multi-file development workflow within a single terminal instance. Covers horizontal and vertical window splits (:sp, :vsp, Ctrl+w navigation), tab page creation and switching (:tabe, gt, gT), and handling open, in-memory file buffers (:ls, :bn, :bp, :bd).

chapters.title: Codebase Navigation & Structure Hooks
chapters.description: Traversing large development projects efficiently. Covers automated code blocks alignment and formatting (=, <, >), quick code folding toggles (zo, zc, za, zM, zR), jumping directly to file paths under the cursor (gf), and jumping to function definitions via tag files (Ctrl+], Ctrl+t).

chapters.title: Linux Shell Integration & Sudo Hacks
chapters.description: Interacting directly with the underlying Linux environment. Covers running arbitrary bash commands without pausing the session (:!), piping external terminal command output directly into the current editing pane (:r !), and rescuing a file when you forgot to open it with root privileges (:w !sudo tee %).

chapters.title: Dev Essential .vimrc Oneliners
chapters.description: A lightweight configuration cheat-sheet. Lists immediate configurations to place inside the ~/.vimrc configuration file to permanently enable line numbers, fix syntax highlighting colors, handle mouse interactions, and prevent automated formatting glitches during text pastes.
